item.title: The AI jobs debate just got messier
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: A new report finds "high-intensity AI adopters” saw headcount increase 10.2%. Among those companies, entry-level headcount rose by 12%, countering the rhetoric that AI kills junior jobs.

item.title: Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Wix-owned vibe coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.

item.title: Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Google is expanding Gemini’s personalized AI image generation to eligible free users in the U.S., allowing the chatbot to create images based on your interests and data from connected Google apps.

item.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.

item.title: South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘RAMageddon’
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: The world's two largest memory chip companies vow to build more memory lab fabs as South Korea positions itself as an AI tech powerhouse country.

item.title: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The startup, which runs a popular free AI leaderboard, launched its commercial service just last September.

item.title: Cursor now has a mobile app for guiding your coding agent on the go
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Cursor has launched a new mobile app for remote oversight over coding agents.

item.title: TIDAL cracks down on AI music by cutting off monetization
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: In addition, TIDAL will use automated tools to remove AI-generated music that attempts to impersonate an artist or a group, the company said.

item.title: Robot hand company settles Tesla trade secret suit and announces $11M raise
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The startup, Proception, is taking a unique approach to collecting training data to tackle one of the hardest problems in robotics: hands.

item.title: Omen AI’s plan to optimize data centers is all wet
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Omen AI raised a $31 million Series A to monitor chip coolant and stop bacterial outbreaks in data centers.

item.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”

item.title: Why Wall Street thinks US memory maker Micron is the next Nvidia
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Eager to find more public AI-related companies that may do as well as Nvidia, Wall Street investors think they've found a winner with Micron.

item.title: SoftBank’s CEO isn’t the only one with questions about Elon Musk’s orbital data center hype
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Not everyone is buying Elon Musk’s vision for orbital data centers.

item.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

item.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: When confronted with cancer, Conno Christou fed everything tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

item.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

item.title: Trump administration releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

item.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request, says restrictions shouldn’t be the norm
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: “We don’t believe this kind of government access process should become the long-term default,” says OpenAI. “It keeps the best tools from users, developers, enterprises, cyber defenders, and global partners who need them.”

item.title: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring.

item.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk. The goal is less of a […]
